details.claim: V0 has 5 active modules: I-1, I-2, I-3, I-5, I-19
details.sot_ref: MASTER_SPEC §2.3 I-Series table: I-1~I-3 CORE ON, I-5 CORE ON, I-19 CORE ON; I-4 CORE ON but PART2 excludes from V0 per READINESS_GUIDE §2.7
details.note: PART2 explicitly notes I-4 excluded from V0; SOT I-Series table shows I-4 as V1 ON (not V0-specific). PART2 V0 scoping is consistent with READINESS_GUIDE.

details.sot_ref: CLAUDE.md L18: not explicitly zero but 'V0(구조기반)'; PART2 L107: '₩0 (로컬)'
details.note: SOT does not specify V0 cost explicitly but no contradiction. PART2 L187 says 'test ~₩5,000'.

details.sot_ref: MASTER_SPEC §15 Tech Stack: V1=SQLite+Chroma; PHASE_B4 §3.4: backend='chroma' LOCK(V1), §3.6: backend='sqlite'

details.claim: V0 LLM: Ollama single (llama3.2:3b mini, llama3.1:8b main)

details.sot_ref: PHASE_B4 §3.1: autonomy_level='L1' LOCK; CLAUDE.md table: V0=L1
MASTER_SPEC §2.3: I-25 SDAR COND, V1=OFF. CLAUDE.md §6: I-25 COND, OFF in V1 (thus OFF in V0)

details.claim: I-8/I-9/I-20 are V0 stubs, full implementation in V1 Phase 1
details.sot_ref: MASTER_SPEC §2.3: I-8 CORE(LOCK) V1=ON, I-9 CORE(LOCK) V1=ON, I-20 CORE V1=ON. Stub in V0 is PART2's implementation decision, consistent with V0 being scaffold only

details.claim: config.v1.toml has 13 sections for V0 (17 for V1+)
details.sot_ref: PHASE_B4 §3: defines 17 sections total. PART2 V0 excludes [blue_nodes],[ui],[rate_limit],[guardrails]
details.note: PHASE_B4 defines 17 sections. V0 subset of 13 is PART2's implementation scoping choice, consistent.

details.claim: V0 embedding model: bge-m3, dimension 1024 (storage), matryoshka 256 (search)
details.sot_ref: PHASE_B4 §3.3: model='bge-m3' LOCK(V1), dimension=1024 LOCK; PHASE_B3 §4.2: FlagEmbedding BGE-M3 1024dim

details.claim: V0 has 25 Pydantic v2 schemas in contracts.py
details.sot_ref: No single SOT specifies exactly 25 for V0. D2.1-D1~D8 define more schemas collectively. PART2 selects 25 as V0 subset.
details.note: The number 25 is PART2's V0 scoping decision. SOT schemas total more. Not contradicted but not explicitly specified as V0=25 in any SOT.

PART2 L581 says 18 (D2.1-D2 §4.1). MASTER_SPEC says 16. Phase 2 finding F-01 already flagged this. D2.1-D2 is higher authority for schema details, but inconsistency between MASTER_SPEC and D2.1-D2 persists.

details.sot_ref: CLAUDE.md §12: ResponseEnvelope 5 fields confirmed. D2.1-D5 §4.9 also defines ResponseEnvelopeSchema owner=D5.
details.note: Field count matches. Source attribution has dual ownership (CLAUDE.md vs D5) per Phase 2 finding F-A5-05, but 5-field count is correct.

details.sot_ref: D2.1-D2 SOT: needs verification of exact counts. MASTER_SPEC §12 mentions EventType registry.
details.note: Exact counts (123/36/23) need D2.1-D2 full read to confirm. PART2 references D2.1-D2 as source. Count accuracy cannot be fully verified without reading D2.1-D2 registry sections.

details.claim: LangGraph StateGraph with 5 nodes: intake, plan, execute, verify, deliver
details.sot_ref: CLAUDE.md §5: 5-Phase Pipeline (LOCK) — Perception/Intake, Reasoning/Plan, Action/Execute, Reflection/Verify, Memory/Store. D2.0-05 §7: standard 5-stage pipeline
details.note: PART2 maps 5-Phase to LangGraph nodes correctly. deliver node = CLAUDE.md Deliver stage.

details.claim: V0 JSON-RPC communication: stdin/stdout between Rust and Python
details.sot_ref: PHASE_B1 §1.3: 'Python-Rust: JSON-RPC over subprocess stdin/stdout (V1)', PHASE_B1 §0: 'JSON-RPC over subprocess (Python-Rust)'

details.claim: LogEventSchema: 7 fields (trace_id, timestamp, level, module, event_type, message, data)
details.sot_ref: D2.1-D2 §4.2: event_type, producer, when, payload, severity, sinks, links — different field names
details.note: Count is same (7) but field names differ significantly. Phase 2 finding F-A5-01 confirmed this. PART2 L1270 provides structlog mapping table resolving this, but initial claim in L582 comment uses non-SOT names.

details.sot_fact: 5-Phase Pipeline is LOCK (Perception/Intake → Reasoning/Plan → Action/Execute → Reflection/Verify → Memory/Store)
5-Gate system: PolicyGate, CostGate, ApprovalGate, EvidenceGate, SelfCheckGate — bypass impossible (LOCK)
Document priority: RULE 1.3 > PLAN 3.0 > DESIGN 2.0 LOCK > DESIGN body > Schema/TECH_STACK
V0 I-1 state machine: I1_S0_RAW → I1_S1_PARSING → I1_S2_AMBIGUOUS(V1+) → I1_S3_READY → I1_S4_EMITTED
ORANGE CORE is judgment/control only, not execution engine
Runnable protocol: all modules must implement run(input)->output, get_metadata(), health_check()
details.note: PART2 does not mention Runnable protocol explicitly. V0 modules use ad-hoc function signatures.

details.sot_fact: VamosError standard: Result<T, VamosError> with failure_code, message, fallback_id, trace_id
details.note: Phase 2 finding F-03 already flagged this. PART2 mentions FailureReport but not VamosError structure.

details.sot_fact: Performance benchmarks: simple response ≤2s, complex ≤10s, self-check ≤1s
details.note: Phase 2 finding F-04 already flagged this. No performance targets in PART2.

details.sot_fact: DEC-001: VAMOS uses own lightweight framework, external agent frameworks (LangChain, CrewAI) import forbidden. LangGraph allowed for orchestration only (DEC-002)
details.part2_ref: L1174 (LangGraph scope limit), L397-398 (langchain-community for Ollama wrapper)

details.sot_fact: 9-State machine: S0_RECEIVED → S1_INTENT_PARSED → S2_EVIDENCE_READY → S3_DECISION_LOCKED → S4_EXECUTING → S5_OUTPUT_READY → S6_SELF_CHECKED → S7_MEMORY_COMMITTED → S8_DONE
S3a_APPROVE_WAIT sub-state for P2 approval (600s timeout)
Docker sandboxing for AI-generated code execution
details.sot_ref: D2.0-02 §1.3-A: all AI code execution in Docker sandbox
details.part2_ref: V2 scope (not V0), but principle acknowledged

details.check: Pydantic v2 with ConfigDict (not class Config)
VamosState TypedDict fields defined for LangGraph StateGraph
details.note: v8 Agent 1 flagged this as BLOCKER (C-16). PART2 now provides VamosState field list in STEP-4 comments (L1128-1136), partially resolving v8's C-16. However, it's still in comments, not a formal Pydantic model definition.

details.check: config.v1.toml semantic_cache.ttl_sec value: PART2 says 86400 citing D2.0-06, PHASE_B4 says 3600
details.note: SOURCE_CONFLICT acknowledged in PART2 (L324 comment). PART2 adopts D2.0-06 value (86400) per DESIGN 2.0 > PHASE_B priority rule. The conflict itself is a real issue between SOT documents.

findings.description: DecisionSchema field count: PART2 says 18 fields (D2.1-D2 §4.1), MASTER_SPEC says 16 fields. Inconsistency between SOT documents persists.
findings.evidence: PART2 L581: '18 (FREEZE)' vs MASTER_SPEC §4.4: '16필드, FREEZE'. Phase 2 F-01 previously identified.

findings.description: LogEventSchema field names in PART2 comment (L582) differ from D2.1-D2 §4.2 SOT field names. PART2 provides mapping table in L1270-1281 but initial reference is misleading.
findings.evidence: PART2 L582: 'trace_id,timestamp,level,module,event_type,message,data'. D2.1-D2: 'event_type,producer,when,payload,severity,sinks,links'. Phase 2 F-A5-01 confirmed SUSTAINED.

findings.description: Runnable protocol (run/get_metadata/health_check interface) from D2.0-02 §1.2-A not mentioned in PART2 V0 implementation steps.
findings.evidence: D2.0-02 §1.2-A defines Runnable protocol as mandatory for all modules. PART2 V0 STEP-4 modules use ad-hoc function signatures without Runnable interface.

findings.description: VamosError standard error handling structure not explicitly defined in PART2. Phase 2 F-03 already identified.
findings.evidence: D2.0-02 §0.3: VamosError with failure_code, message, fallback_id, trace_id. PART2 uses FailureReport but not VamosError.

findings.description: Performance benchmarks (simple ≤2s, complex ≤10s, self-check ≤1s) from MASTER_SPEC §4.8 absent from PART2. Phase 2 F-04 already identified.
findings.evidence: MASTER_SPEC §4.8 defines 5 performance targets not in PART2.

findings.description: VamosState defined only in comments (STEP-4 L1128-1136), not as formal Pydantic/TypedDict model. Partially resolves v8 BLOCKER C-16 but formal definition still needed.
findings.evidence: PART2 L1128-1136: VamosState fields listed in Python comments. No formal model class definition.

findings.description: SOURCE_CONFLICT: semantic_cache.ttl_sec = 86400 (D2.0-06) vs 3600 (PHASE_B4). PART2 correctly applies DESIGN > PHASE priority rule but conflict between SOT docs is unresolved.
findings.evidence: PART2 L324/L500: ttl_sec=86400 with SOURCE_CONFLICT comment.

resolved: v8 C-16 (VamosState fields) — partially resolved: PART2 now provides field list in STEP-4 comments (L1128-1136)

v8 C-11 (5-Phase to 9-State mapping) — not V0-critical, V1 scope
